insight.headline: Storefront API update
insight.what_changed: The Storefront API now supports the @inContext directive with an optional channelId argument, allowing developers to apply a specific sales channel's context to an entire query. This change is available in Storefront API version 2026-10. The @inContext directive can be used to fetch data specific to a particular sales channel.
insight.developer_impact: Developers should update their Storefront API queries to include the channelId argument when necessary. This change enables more flexibility in querying sales channel-specific data. Developers using the Storefront API should review their code and update it to take advantage of this new feature. The GraphQL Admin API and Hydrogen can be used in conjunction with the Storefront API to build more robust and sales channel-aware applications.
insight.merchant_impact: Merchants will benefit from more accurate and sales channel-specific data in their Storefront API-powered applications. This change enables developers to build more tailored and sales channel-aware experiences for merchants.
insight.why_it_matters: This update matters because it provides more flexibility and control over sales channel-specific data, enabling developers to build more robust and tailored applications for merchants. It also highlights Shopify's commitment to improving the Storefront API and providing more features for developers.

also_worth_knowing.text: Metafield triggers and additional topics are now available for Events
Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials
Full-stack capabilities to power app analytics

updates.title: Metafield triggers and additional topics are now available for Events
updates.summary: Metafield triggers and additional topics are now available for Events, allowing apps to subscribe to events for various resources and metafield changes.

updates.title: Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials
updates.summary: Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials, simplifying theme page building.

updates.title: Full-stack capabilities to power app analytics
updates.summary: Full-stack capabilities are now available to power app analytics, simplifying the process of building merchant-facing analytics.

updates.title: Physical inventory feature preview
updates.summary: The physical inventory feature preview is now available, providing early access to new inventory APIs in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API.
CLI version 4.5.2 fixes a warning when running theme commands in valid theme directories and bumps Shopify/theme-tools packages.

updates.title: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate
updates.summary: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, affecting apps that store cardholder data.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2
updates.summary: Hydrogen CLI version 13.0.2 adds non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags and allows bypassing confirmation prompts in hydrogen upgrade.

updates.title: Updated App Store requirements: 4.1.2 Use a unique name for your app
updates.summary: Updated App Store requirements include using a unique name for your app to reduce merchant confusion.

updates.title: Storefront API `@inContext` supports `channelId`
updates.summary: The Storefront API @inContext directive now supports channelId, allowing for channel-specific context in queries.

updates.title: `productId`, `title`,`variantSku`, and `variantTitle` fields added to `ExchangeLineItem`
updates.summary: The ExchangeLineItem type now includes productId, title, variantSku, and variantTitle fields.

updates.title: POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components
updates.summary: POS UI Extensions API version 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components, providing more detailed discount information.
CLI version 4.5.1 bumps cli-hydrogen to 13.0.1, providing updates and improvements.

recap.headline: Shopify updates include new event triggers, Liquid template improvements, and physical inventory feature preview

themes.title: Event Triggers and Webhooks
themes.detail: New event triggers and topics are available for subscription, including metafield changes on Product, Order, Customer, Collection, and Location. This update enables more flexible and automated workflows for apps using the GraphQL Admin API and Webhooks.

themes.title: Theme Development and Liquid
themes.detail: The Liquid July ’26 developer preview introduces a simpler way to build theme pages with blocks and partials, making it easier to compose and edit page structures directly in Liquid templates. Additionally, the CLI version 4.5.2 includes fixes and improvements for theme development.

themes.title: App Development and Security
themes.detail: The physical inventory feature preview is now available in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API, allowing developers to build and test against new inventory APIs. Furthermore, the card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, enhancing security for apps handling customer payment methods.

actions.text: Update apps to use new event triggers and metafield subscriptions
Test physical inventory feature preview in development stores
Implement mTLS client certificate for card deposit endpoint
